definition: vpc resolves branches that require both I2S literal substitution for a terminal gate-byte/literal AND VP gradient feedback to navigate upstream multi-byte or sequential equality gates; neither technique alone suffices within budget.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes the 4-byte 'CFF2' table-tag literal from intercepted CMP operands; VP gradient preserves seeds making incremental progress toward a sanitizable CFF2 top-dict; only their union clears both tag and header stages within budget.
signature.one_line: I2S plants CFF2 tag; VP gradient scaffolds multi-byte header validation

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must form a valid TTF wrapper carrying a CFF2-tagged subtable whose blob survives hb_blob_t::as<cff2>() sanitization.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A TTF/OpenType container with the 'CFF2' four-byte table tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f, a sfnt header magic '00 01 00 00' at offset 0x00-0x03, a one-entry table directory (numTables=1) and a CFF2 subtable whose header bytes ('01 00 02 0c ...' starting around offset 0x18) parse as a valid CFF2 top-dict so that hb_blob_t::as<OT::cff2>() returns a non-Null pointer at line 409.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five W seeds carry the exact byte pattern '00 01 00 00 00 01 ... CFF2 ... 00 00 00 1a 01 00 02 0c' in the first 0x1c bytes, giving a sfnt header with numTables=1, a CFF2 tag, and a CFF2 top-dict that sanitizes; the W-side overlay shows execution proceeding past line 411 into the topDict parsing block at lines 414-420.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: L seeds are scattered at the magic-byte offsets (offset 0x0c-0x0f shows 00/20/56/56 'VV', 'VA', 'GS', 'GP' table tags instead of 'CFF2'; offset 0x05 ranges across 04/20/1f/03 instead of 01); the L-side overlay shows control flow hits line 412 'goto fail' for 9/10 trials, so the cff2 pointer compared equal to &Null and the constructor aborts before topDict.init.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Synergy of I2SRandReplace (cmplog/vpc) substituting the literal 'CFF2' four-byte tag into the input from intercepted CMPs against the table-directory iterator, AND the CMP_MAP Hamming-distance gradient (vp/vpc) preserving intermediate seeds that get progressively closer to a sanitizable CFF2 top-dict structure. cmplog alone can plant 'CFF2' but cannot incrementally satisfy the multi-byte CFF2 header validation; value_profile alone has the gradient but no literal substitution for the tag — only their union (vpc) clears both stages within the 12h budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality comparison of cff2 against the sentinel &Null(OT::cff2) at line 411 in the cff2 accelerator constructor.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Source line:       if (cff2 == &Null (OT::cff2))

evidence_trail.claim: The winner-resolving seeds share an identical 16-byte prefix containing a sfnt header and the 'CFF2' table tag.
The 'CFF2' tag bytes at offsets 0x0c-0x0f are fully concentrated in W (all 10 reads = 43 46 46 32) but diverge in L (mostly 'VV', 'VA', 'GS', 'GP' or 00/20).
Offset 0x0f is uniformly '32' (ASCII '2') in W seeds but '56' (V) or 00 in L seeds.
Loser seeds branch into line 412 'goto fail' (9 of 10 L trials reach line 411 True) whereas winner seeds skip that goto and execute the topDict parsing block (W hits lines 414-420 exclusively).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 411  T=0 F=10  T=9 F=0  if (cff2 == &Null (OT::cff2))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: OT::cff2::sanitize is invoked 10 times by W seeds and 0 times by L seeds — only W seeds reach the CFF2 sanitization codepath that gates the as<cff2>() success.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 10         0  OT::cff2::sanitize(hb_sanitize_context_t*) const  (/src/harfbuzz/src/hb-ot-cff2-table.hh:388-392)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs at this branch are synergy losses — neither cmplog (with I2S) nor value_profile (with CMP_MAP) resolves any of 10 trials within the campaign, while value_profile_cmplog (with both) resolves 10/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=0/10  blocked=10  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace works by scanning the input for one CMP operand and substituting the other — the mechanism that can plant the 'CFF2' four-byte literal into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP bucket signal preserves seeds whose CMP operands are coming progressively closer to matching — the gradient mechanism that helps stage-by-stage past CFF2 header sub-checks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which the gate is `memcmp(input+12, "CFF2", 4) == 0` followed by an inequality check on a small computed length field, where cmplog alone resolves at the same rate as value_profile_cmplog — this would show I2S substitution by itself is sufficient and the value_profile gradient is not a co-requisite, refuting the synergy claim.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The 'CMP_MAP gradient is a co-requisite' half of the synergy claim is the weakest leg — the prompt shows cmplog losing 10/10 but does not directly demonstrate which downstream sub-CMP (e.g., topDictSize, FDSelect offset) is the gradient-friendly stage that vp+I2S clears together, so the gradient contribution is inferred from the BBR shape rather than observed in seed bytes.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S plants the small equation-type integer constants (0–4) into the single pCAL type byte; VP gradient navigates upstream chunk and CRC gates to reach the pCAL handler; only vpc combining both avoids the ARBITRARY+mismatched-nparams early-return.
signature.one_line: I2S lands equation-type byte; VP gradient reaches pCAL handler

analysis.summary_one_line: PNG file reaching the pCAL handler with a type byte whose value is not PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (commonly type>=PNG_EQUATION_LAST so the whole nparams-mismatch OR-chain evaluates false).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG stream containing a syntactically valid pCAL chunk that survives CRC and length checks, where the equation type byte at pCAL_payload+9 satisfies the OR-chain's false branch — i.e. type is not PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (2) and not one of the other recognized values with mismatched nparams. In the winner seeds, the type byte additionally satisfies type >= PNG_EQUATION_LAST (4), which is why the else-if at L2329 only fires in W.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog seeds reach OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_pCAL (entry-line counts diverge sharply in W vs L) and at the type byte the W cohort never matches PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L2322 shows W T=0 F=22 for type==ARBITRARY) and proceeds into the else-if at L2329 that is hit only in W. vpc combines CMP_MAP gradient feedback (which guides toward valid chunk structure / past CRC/length gates) with I2SRandReplace substitution that can plant the small literal constants compared against type (0,1,2,3,4) directly into pCAL_payload+9, so vpc finds both the navigation and the gate-byte in one strategy.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2SRandReplace but lacks CMP_MAP gradient, so it explores the upstream PNG chunk soup more sparsely and tends to land on pCAL payloads with type==PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L2322 shows L T=11 F=0 for type==ARBITRARY combined with nparams!=3, triggering the L2325 'invalid parameter count' early-return). value_profile has CMP_MAP gradient and can navigate to the pCAL handler but without I2SRandReplace it cannot cheaply substitute the equation-type constants into the type byte, so it spends time on the same ARBITRARY+mismatched-nparams pattern and times out. naive lacks both and barely reaches the chunk (REFERENCE, 1/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace is the proximate technique that lands the pCAL type byte on a value satisfying the OR-chain's false side. The cmplog/vpc CmpLogObserver buffer captures the equation-type literal operands at the L2320-2323 comparisons and I2SRandReplace substitutes them into the input at the byte that flows into 'type = buf[9]'. The synergy that distinguishes vpc from cmplog (Pair 1's value_profile delta) is the value-profile distance-bucket feedback that preserves seeds making incremental progress through upstream byte-equality gates so that pCAL is reached often enough for I2SRandReplace to fire on the right offsets. Lineage of winner seed 04527fb15010de2b shows an I2S-floor (mutation_op=-) ancestor at depth 20, consistent with I2SRandReplace being on the path.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the OR-chained equation-type/nparams check at pngrutil.c:2322 inside OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_pCAL with globally blocked side False.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Location: /src/libpng/pngrutil.c:2322:43
Enclosing function: OSS_FUZZ_png_handle_pCAL
Source line:        (type == PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY && nparams != 3) ||
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog is the sole winner; it beats both cmplog (delta value_profile) and value_profile (delta I2S), while naive is a reference fuzzer.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive                            1        9          0  REFERENCE
cmplog                           2        8          0  loser (value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog)
value_profile                    2        8          0  loser (I2S vs value_profile_cmplog)
value_profile_cmplog             8        2          0  winner (value_profile vs cmplog); winner (I2S vs value_profile)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs are admissible and both have vpc as winner — Pair 1 (vpc>cmplog, value_profile) and Pair 2 (vpc>value_profile, I2S).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
--- Pair 1: value_profile_cmplog > cmplog  [delta: value_profile] ---
  subject 11  (value_profile_cmplog vs cmplog, admissible)

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 2 (vpc>value_profile, delta I2S) is admissible with strong subject-level significance.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
--- Pair 2: value_profile_cmplog > value_profile  [delta: I2S] ---
  subject 12  (value_profile_cmplog vs value_profile, admissible)

evidence_trail.claim: The source at the blocker is one disjunct of a 4-way OR-chain comparing the parsed type byte against PNG_EQUATION_* literal constants.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
[B]  2320     if ((type == PNG_EQUATION_LINEAR && nparams != 2) ||
[B]  2321         (type == PNG_EQUATION_BASE_E && nparams != 3) ||
[B]  2322         (type == PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY && nparams != 3) || <-- BLOCKER
[B]  2323         (type == PNG_EQUATION_HYPERBOLIC && nparams != 4))

evidence_trail.claim: The type byte is read from pCAL chunk payload offset 9 (and nparams from offset 10); these are 1-byte fields directly read from the input.
The else-if at L2329 testing type>=PNG_EQUATION_LAST is hit only in winner-resolving seeds — i.e., W seeds drive the type byte into the >=LAST regime.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: [W]  2329     else if (type >= PNG_EQUATION_LAST)

evidence_trail.claim: DIVERGENT BRANCHES shows W seeds never match type==PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY (W T=0 F=22) while L seeds always match it (L T=11 F=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
d=1   L2322  T=0 F=22  T=11 F=0  (type == PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY && nparams != 3) ||  <-- BLOCKER
  d=1   L2322  T=22 F=0  T=11 F=0  (type == PNG_EQUATION_ARBITRARY && nparams != 3) ||  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: L seeds never reach the post-error code (the for-loop at L2334, the params malloc at L2342, the nparams loop at L2349); W seeds do — the loser's pCAL handler returns early at the invalid-parameter-count error at L2325.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
d=1   L2334  T=118 F=22  T=0 F=0  for (buf = units; *buf; buf++)
  d=1   L2342  T=0 F=22  T=0 F=0  if (params == NULL)
  d=1   L2349  T=66 F=22  T=0 F=0  for (i = 0; i < nparams; i++)

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds were discovered by cmplog (the seed_bisect-picked direction); their mutation_op chains include I2S-floor parents in lineage, supporting I2SRandReplace contribution.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=04527fb15010de2b, size=3152 bytes, fuzzer=cmplog, trial=1, discovered_at=1599s, mutation_op=BytesInsertCopyMutator,ByteFlipMutator,BitFlipMutator,BytesDeleteMutator)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's mutator stack includes I2SRandReplace, which scans input bytes for an intercepted CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace; it has only CMP_MAP gradient feedback over havoc/token mutations.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack. No `I2SRandReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog's mutator stack is the union — it carries both CMP_MAP gradient feedback and I2SRandReplace.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: edge-bucket + CMP_MAP-bucket signals.

**Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient produces distance-bucketed feedback over CMPs so an input closer to matching is preserved as corpus, which helps navigate upstream byte-equality gates.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: edge-bucket signal **plus** new-CMP_MAP-bucket signal
(both via `MaxMapFeedback`-style coverage). An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: Hit-count divergence shows L heavily exercises downstream IDAT/row-read paths (W=0) while W exercises code reachable from the pCAL handler past the error (PLTE, iCCP, zTXt), consistent with the L vs W split being 'L returned early from the pCAL invalid-parameter-count error vs W continued through'.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
0      2160  OSS_FUZZ_png_read_finish_row  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:4328-4388)
       0       338  OSS_FUZZ_png_read_IDAT_data  (/src/libpng/pngrutil.c:4152-4276)

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic experiment where the pCAL type-byte gate is preserved but every upstream equality CMP (PNG signature, IHDR magic, chunk-type FOURCCs, CRC) is replaced with a memcmp-free / non-instrumentable check shows cmplog catching up to vpc (closing the value_profile-delta gap). That would mean the value_profile component was carrying navigation rather than the gate itself, but the gate was solvable by cmplog alone — i.e., the synergy is not real and the shape collapses to a single-technique story.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The BYTE DIFF section only covers the first 64 bytes of each seed (PNG header + IHDR/gAMA/sRGB area) and does not include the pCAL chunk payload where the type byte at payload+9 actually sits, so direct byte-level evidence that the type byte differs in the predicted way (W: type>=4, L: type==2) is not present in this prompt — the type/nparams claim is inferred from DIVERGENT BRANCHES counts and the SOURCE CONTEXT overlay rather than visible in the hex dumps.

signature.mechanism_summary: I2S substitutes both the 9-byte '<![CDATA[' opener and the 3-byte ']]>' terminator from CMP operands; VP gradient retains partial-prefix progress through the byte-equality cascade gating CDATA state entry; neither technique alone suffices.
signature.one_line: I2S supplies CDATA literals; VP gradient holds partial cascade progress

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain both the 9-byte '<![CDATA[' opener and a '<' / structural prefix so the parser enters CDATA state and finds the ']]>' terminator.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must (a) navigate the push-parser through XML_PARSER_CONTENT to the 9-byte equality cascade '<![CDATA[' on lines 11747-11754, which sets ctxt->instate = XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION; AND (b) include a subsequent ']]>' 3-byte literal so that xmlParseLookupString / strstr at lines 11825-11828 returns a non-NULL pointer, taking the False branch of `if (term == NULL)`.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog produces inputs in which both '<![CDATA[' and ']]>' appear at corpus-discovery time. Synergy: only the fuzzer carrying BOTH I2SRandReplace (for substituting multi-byte literal operands like the 9-byte CDATA opener and the 3-byte ']]>') AND CMP_MAP gradient (for incrementally navigating preceding multi-byte equality cascades that gate state entry) clears the gate; the W seed's body confirms presence of these substrings.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser of pair 1) has I2SRandReplace but no CMP_MAP gradient, so it cannot retain partial-prefix progress on the long byte cascades that gate entry into CDATA state — it blocks 10/10 trials. value_profile (loser of pair 2) has CMP_MAP gradient but lacks I2SRandReplace, so even when its gradient navigates into the CDATA section, it cannot synthesize the literal ']]>' terminator from CMP operands — it blocks 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Joint mechanism (synergy / 'other'): the multi-byte equality cascade gating XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION state plus the literal-terminator search both demand I2S-style operand substitution AND CMP_MAP-style partial-match feedback. Neither single technique suffices — only their union (value_profile_cmplog) resolves the branch.

evidence_trail.claim: Blocker is `if (term == NULL)` at parser.c:11831 inside the XML_PARSER_CDATA_SECTION case of xmlParseTryOrFinish, and the winner takes the False branch.
evidence_trail.cited_locator: Location, Source line, Globally blocked side
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line: 		if (term == NULL) {
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Reaching the CDATA case requires the 9-byte equality cascade '<![CDATA[' to fire in XML_PARSER_CONTENT.
Inside the CDATA case, term is set by strstr(...,"]]>") (terminate) or xmlParseLookupString(...,"]]>",3) (push), so the False branch requires the literal three-byte ']]>' to be present.
Two decisive pairs share the same winner value_profile_cmplog: one delta is value_profile (vs cmplog), the other delta is I2S (vs value_profile) — implying both techniques are required.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: --- Pair 1: value_profile_cmplog > cmplog  [delta: value_profile] ---

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 2 is the I2S delta: vpc resolves 10/10 while value_profile resolves only 2/10.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: --- Pair 2: value_profile_cmplog > value_profile  [delta: I2S] ---

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog blocks all 10 trials (winner of NO pair, loser of pair 1).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: cmplog                           0       10          0  loser (value_profile vs value_profile_cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile blocks 8/10 trials with only 2 resolutions, losing the I2S-delta pair to vpc.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: value_profile                    2        8          0  loser (I2S vs value_profile_cmplog)

evidence_trail.claim: vpc resolves 10/10 trials.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: value_profile_cmplog            10        0          0  winner (value_profile vs cmplog); winner (I2S vs value_profile)

evidence_trail.claim: Loser trials hit xmlParseTryOrFinish many more times (57) than winner trials (6), suggesting they re-enter the parser loop without ever transitioning past the CDATA gate.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: 6        57  parser.c:xmlParseTryOrFinish  (/src/libxml2/parser.c:11404-12120)  <-- enclosing

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line, losers take the True branch 44 times and the False branch 0 times (never find ']]>'), while winners take True 5 / False 1 (find ']]>' at least once).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L11831  T=5 F=1  T=44 F=0  if (term == NULL) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's I2SRandReplace mutator substitutes byte sequences matching one operand of a CMP with the other operand, which is the canonical mechanism for synthesizing literal byte runs such as the 9-byte CDATA opener and 3-byte ']]>' terminator.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient rewards inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching, providing the partial-prefix feedback that helps drive bytewise convergence on long equality cascades.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog combines both techniques into a single fuzzer, which is the only configuration that resolves the branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Mutators**: naive's havoc + token stack **plus** `I2SRandReplace`.

evidence_trail.claim: Even the winning W seed body contains the literal '<![CDATA[' opener and ']]>' terminator (and so satisfies the source-line gates).
evidence_trail.cited_locator: winner-resolving seed 24a170e885c303ff (first 64 bytes shown; full payload visible via db_query more-seeds)
evidence_trail.exact_quote: Seed 1 (id=24a170e885c303ff, size=638 bytes, fuzzer=value_profile, trial=5, discovered_at=13194s, mutation_op=TokenReplace):

evidence_trail.claim: The W seed's first 64 bytes are mangled into a run of '=' bytes between '<!DO' and 'CTYPE', showing the mutator stream has been heavily perturbed in the prefix yet still yields a corpus-resolving input — consistent with multi-stage mutation accumulating downstream CDATA tokens.

BYTE DIFF shows W and L differ across the entire visible 64-byte prefix, with W constant on each offset (W bytes set has size 1 everywhere) but L containing the literal token '<?xml version="1' / 'DOCTYPE' — i.e., the L corpus is converging on document-prolog content while W has carried forward a CDATA-bearing payload at a later offset.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness gating on a `<![CDATA[ ... ]]>` literal cascade (9-byte opener + 3-byte terminator) shows cmplog OR value_profile (alone) resolving at parity with vpc — i.e., either single technique suffices on the isolated pattern — then the joint-mechanism / synergy story is wrong and the branch must depend on additional state machinery upstream rather than on the dual-token requirement.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Only ONE winner-resolving seed is recorded in the DB, and it is tagged value_profile (not vpc), even though vpc resolves 10/10 and value_profile only 2/10 — seed_bisect's lex-min picker happened to land on a vp trial here, so the byte-diff and lineage evidence is necessarily one-sided and cannot directly demonstrate which mutator chain vpc used.
